tools.name: ImageDescription
tools.description: A useful tool that returns a brief description of the input image.

tools.name: OCR
tools.description: This tool can recognize all text on the input image.

outputs.description: OCR results, include bbox in x1, y1, x2, y2 format and the recognized text.

tools.name: RegionAttributeDescription
tools.description: Describe the attribute of a region of the input image.

inputs.name: bbox
inputs.description: The bbox coordinate in the format of `(x1, y1, x2, y2)`

dialogs.content: In the image determine which information is summarized?

dialogs.thought: First orient to the figure type and overall content (flowchart/tree summarizing cohort and test results).

content.content: A black-and-white flowchart/tree diagram labeled “Study group n=166” that branches by surgical histology (hist− vs hist+), then by nodal stage (stage 1, stage 2, stage 3), then by ultrasound node status (US node+ vs US node−), and then by whether ultrasound-guided biopsy was performed (USB vs no USB) with terminal counts including M, B, and SE categories.

dialogs.thought: Extract the figure labels to confirm it summarizes surgical histology, nodal stage, ultrasound visibility, and US-guided core biopsy outcomes with patient counts.

arguments.attribute: what clinical variables and outcomes are being stratified and counted in this region

dialogs.thought: Verify in the main branching region that the figure is a cohort summary linking surgical histology/nodal stage with ultrasound node visibility and biopsy results.

content.content: The region shows a branching cohort diagram with patient counts stratified by nodal stage at surgery (stage 1/2/3), ultrasound node status (US node+ vs US node−), whether ultrasound-guided biopsy was done (USB vs no USB), and terminal biopsy outcome categories labeled M, B, and SE with corresponding n values.

dialogs.content: The purpose of the figure is to present a summary of the surgical histology and preoperative ultrasound (US) core biopsy results for a group of patients. It shows the number of patients with different outcomes, such as the presence or absence of malignant nodes on surgical histology, the nodal stage at surgery, the visibility of nodes on ultrasound, and the results of the US core biopsy. This information helps to understand the correlation between preoperative ultrasound findings, US-guided core biopsy results, and the actual nodal stage at surgery.

whitelist: The purpose of the figure is to present a summary of the surgical histology and preoperative ultrasound (US) core biopsy results for a group of patients. It shows the number of patients with different outcomes, such as the presence or absence of malignant nodes on surgical histology, the nodal stage at surgery, the visibility of nodes on ultrasound, and the results of the US core biopsy. This information helps to understand the correlation between preoperative ultrasound findings, US-guided core biopsy results, and the actual nodal stage at surgery.
